question: Which record controls an access review policy decision on 2026-07-01?

controlling_artifact.title: Approved Export Of Quarterly Access Review Policy

selection_reasons: The policy object is active on 2026-07-01.
The policy object applies to the requested knowledge domain, jurisdiction, and capability.
The Synthetic Policy Standards Board is the authority permitted to approve access review policy requirements.
Policy version 2 supersedes policy version 1 for decisions on or after 2026-01-01.
The approved export represents the active policy object and permits policy interpretation, control requirement evaluation, and workflow selection in this synthetic example.

excluded_candidates.title: AI Generated Summary Of Quarterly Access Review Policy
excluded_because: It is a non-authoritative derivative summary.
It prohibits policy interpretation, control requirement evaluation, operational execution, and production decisioning.
It has no verification record showing approval as a policy source.

excluded_candidates.title: Obsolete Collaboration Copy Of Access Review Policy
excluded_because: It represents policy-access-review-v1-synthetic, which was superseded by policy-access-review-v2-synthetic on 2026-01-01.
Its permissions prohibit policy interpretation and control requirement evaluation.

excluded_candidates.title: Generated Quarterly Access Review Operating Procedure
excluded_because: It is pending approval and has authority_status unverified.
It was derived from a non-authoritative AI summary.
It requires separate approval before it can govern operational execution.

excluded_candidates.title: Verified Collaboration Copy Of Quarterly Access Review Policy
excluded_because: It is a verified replica stored in a collaboration repository, not the controlling source location.
The matching fingerprint verifies byte identity with the approved export but does not transfer source authority to the storage location.

resolution_rules_applied: Identify the requested knowledge domain and decision type.
Filter candidates by jurisdiction and applicability.
Filter candidates by effective date and lifecycle status.
Require an authority permitted to assert the record.
Resolve supersession before considering copies or derivatives.
Block or escalate if unresolved conflicts remain.
Return the selected record and explain excluded alternatives.
notes: This example does not claim factual truth. It shows how governed metadata can expose which synthetic record controls a scoped decision.
